prompt_config.theme: Late Cretaceous Period - Hell Creek Formation (~66 million years ago)
prompt_config.photography_style: National Geographic quality, Prehistoric Planet documentary style

main_prompt: Cinematic wildlife travel photography of the Late Cretaceous Period Hell Creek Formation, 9:16 vertical portrait format, luxury campaign style, golden hour lighting casting warm amber glow, a massive Tyrannosaurus Rex with thick scaly skin towering dramatically in vertical frame, Triceratops and Edmontosaurus herd in mid-ground among prehistoric vegetation, lush fern-covered ground with ancient conifers and Cycads creating vertical depth, giant Quetzalcoatlus pterosaur soaring above, humid subtropical atmosphere with heat haze, professional color grading with rich earthy tones, shot on medium format camera with beautiful vertical depth of field, Prehistoric Planet documentary realism, National Geographic quality, aspirational mood, scientifically accurate, vibrant but natural colors, 8K ultra detailed, immersive vertical storytelling for Stories and Reels, luxury nature documentary aesthetic

composition_notes.stories_reels_optimization: Vertical 9:16 format perfect for immersive mobile viewing, emphasizes towering height of dinosaurs especially T-Rex and Quetzalcoatlus, creates dramatic sense of prehistoric scale
composition_notes.vertical_storytelling: Composition guides eye from foreground fern-covered ground upward through prehistoric fauna to sky, creating journey through Cretaceous ecosystem
framing_options: Option 1: Ground-level perspective looking up at towering T-Rex, ferns in sharp foreground, T-Rex dominating vertical frame against golden sky
Option 2: Vertical slice of prehistoric ecosystem - foreground Triceratops, mid-ground ancient forest, background volcano with haze
Option 3: Giant Quetzalcoatlus standing on ground showing full giraffe-like height in vertical frame, prehistoric landscape around
Option 4: Herd of Edmontosaurus in foreground with ancient conifers towering vertically, creating depth from ground to treetops
composition_notes.mobile_viewing: Optimized for full-screen vertical mobile experience, immersive prehistoric wildlife documentary storytelling
composition_notes.safe_zones: Important dinosaur elements positioned to avoid Stories/Reels UI at top (~250px) and bottom (~310px)

time_period_specifics.years_ago: Approximately 66 million years ago, final age of non-avian dinosaurs
time_period_specifics.climate: Greenhouse Earth - subtropical, humid, warm climate with no polar ice caps

dinosaur_species.tyrannosaurus_rex: T-Rex towering in vertical frame, massive muscular body 12-13m long, thick scaly skin, lips covering teeth when mouth closed, powerful stance emphasizing height and dominance, brown-grey with possible head display colors
dinosaur_species.triceratops: Three-horned herbivore with massive frill, tank-like body, positioned in mid-ground for scale reference, earthy brown-grey base with potential bright frill accents
dinosaur_species.quetzalcoatlus: Giant pterosaur perfect for vertical composition, showing full height from ground (giraffe-tall) or wingspan in flight, long toothless beak, impressive scale
dinosaur_species.edmontosaurus: Duck-billed dinosaurs in herds, grazing on ferns, peaceful behavior, brown-beige coloration, good for showing ecosystem diversity
dinosaur_species.scientific_accuracy: All Hell Creek coexisting species, modern paleontological understanding, no Jurassic anachronisms

prehistoric_vegetation.ground_cover: NO GRASS - ground entirely covered with dense ferns (bracken ferns, tree ferns), creating unique prehistoric ground texture visible in foreground
prehistoric_vegetation.trees_vertical: Tall ancient Araucaria conifers emphasizing vertical composition, ancient pines, short Cycads at ground level, early flowering Magnolia trees
prehistoric_vegetation.landscape_layers: Vertical depth from fern-covered ground through forest mid-story to canopy and sky, creating immersive prehistoric environment
prehistoric_vegetation.visual_texture: Lush alien prehistoric feel, ferns everywhere, browns and prehistoric greens creating unique Cretaceous signature

golden_hour_lighting.light_quality: Warm amber and golden tones with long vertical shadows emphasizing dinosaur height and tree scale
golden_hour_lighting.light_on_dinosaurs: Thick scaly skin catching warm light with dramatic highlights and shadow detail showing texture, backlit pterosaur wings
golden_hour_lighting.light_on_vegetation: Ferns and ancient trees bathed in golden glow, light filtering through conifer branches and creating vertical god rays
golden_hour_lighting.atmospheric_quality: Humid subtropical haze creating vertical depth layers, heat shimmer adding to prehistoric atmosphere
golden_hour_lighting.sky: Warm peachy-pink and lavender gradient at horizon visible in upper portion of vertical frame, subtropical color palette
golden_hour_lighting.vertical_gradient: Natural light gradient from rich warm tones at ground level to softer sky tones above

color_palette.dinosaur_coloration: Scientifically plausible base colors (brown, grey, olive) with vibrant accent colors (reds, oranges, blues) on crests, frills, display features for sexual selection
color_palette.vegetation_palette: Rich prehistoric greens (darker, more blue-green), browns of ancient bark and fern stems, occasional bright white-pink Magnolia blooms
color_palette.color_grading_style: Professional wildlife documentary grading optimized for mobile vertical viewing, National Geographic aesthetic, enhanced warm golden hour tones
color_palette.stories_appeal: Colors that capture attention in fast-scrolling Stories while maintaining documentary realism and scientific accuracy
color_palette.vertical_harmony: Color palette flowing through vertical composition from earthy ground tones to sky pastels

camera_technical.camera_type: Medium format camera aesthetic (Hasselblad wildlife quality)
camera_technical.documentary_style: Prehistoric Planet (Apple TV+) photorealism standard adapted for vertical mobile format
camera_technical.depth_of_field: Vertical wildlife photography DOF, foreground ferns or dinosaur in sharp focus, mid-ground detailed, background with elegant bokeh
camera_technical.focal_length_feel: Wildlife telephoto for dinosaur portraits (200-400mm) or wider for ecosystem establishing shots (24-70mm), emphasizing vertical drama
camera_technical.sharpness: 8K ultra detailed where in focus - every scale, skin fold, fern frond, tree bark texture visible
camera_technical.image_quality: Documentary quality optimized for mobile display, clean with subtle organic texture

composition.layout: Cinematic 9:16 vertical composition emphasizing height and scale of prehistoric megafauna
composition.foreground: Sharp detailed foreground with ferns, muddy ground, or dinosaur at ground level creating immediate engagement
composition.midground: Main subjects - T-Rex towering, Triceratops herd, ancient forest, showing ecosystem layers
composition.background: Sky, distant volcanic peaks with haze, soft bokeh creating vertical atmospheric depth
composition.vertical_flow: Composition guides viewer from prehistoric ground through fauna and flora to sky, immersive vertical journey
composition.scale_emphasis: Vertical format naturally emphasizes towering height of dinosaurs and ancient trees

mood_atmosphere_mood.aspirational: Luxury time-travel experience, making viewer want to witness this lost world, evoking profound sense of wonder
mood_atmosphere_mood.scientific_wonder: Authentic paleontological accuracy meets stunning mobile-optimized visual storytelling
mood_atmosphere_mood.dramatic: Epic scale and drama of prehistoric megafauna emphasized by vertical composition
mood_atmosphere_mood.immersive: Full-screen vertical format creates immersive prehistoric wildlife experience
mood_atmosphere_mood.adventurous: Sense of exploration and discovery in alien yet familiar ancient world
mood_atmosphere_mood.educational: Documentary realism makes content shareable and educational

national_geographic_prehistoric_planet_q.documentary_standards: Prehistoric Planet level scientific accuracy and visual fidelity adapted for vertical Stories/Reels format
national_geographic_prehistoric_planet_q.scientific_accuracy: Modern paleontological understanding - lipped theropods, accurate anatomy, Hell Creek ecosystem accuracy
national_geographic_prehistoric_planet_q.technical_excellence: Perfect photorealistic rendering optimized for mobile vertical viewing
national_geographic_prehistoric_planet_q.wildlife_aesthetic: Captured like modern wildlife photography but of extinct animals, vertical documentary storytelling
national_geographic_prehistoric_planet_q.visual_impact: Immediately captivating vertical imagery worthy of nature documentary or luxury time-travel campaign

negative_prompt: modern animals, humans, contemporary elements, anachronistic dinosaurs (Stegosaurus, Brachiosaurus, Jurassic species), cartoon style, Jurassic Park outdated look, teeth protruding from closed mouth, excessive feathering on large theropods, bright neon colors, grass on ground, modern plants, buildings, vehicles, modern technology, oversaturated cartoon colors, low resolution, blurry, poor CGI, fake look, toy-like, anthropomorphic, inaccurate anatomy, mixed time periods, flat lighting, horizontal composition, landscape orientation

alternative_prompts.option_1_trex_towering: Luxury wildlife photography, 9:16 vertical, golden hour, ground-level perspective looking up at massive Tyrannosaurus Rex towering in frame, thick scaly skin in sharp focus, lips covering teeth, ferns in foreground, warm amber lighting, Haussmannian buildings flanking, professional documentary color grading, medium format quality, Prehistoric Planet realism, Stories optimized, 8K detailed, scientifically accurate Late Cretaceous
alternative_prompts.option_2_ecosystem_layers: Cinematic nature documentary, 9:16 vertical, golden hour Late Cretaceous, vertical slice showing prehistoric ecosystem layers, foreground: Triceratops in fern prairie sharp focus, mid-ground: tall ancient Araucaria conifers, background: volcanic peaks with atmospheric haze, professional wildlife grading, Hell Creek accuracy, immersive vertical storytelling, Reels format, 8K ultra detailed
alternative_prompts.option_3_pterosaur_height: High-end nature photography, 9:16 vertical, golden hour lighting, giant Quetzalcoatlus pterosaur standing on ground showing impressive full giraffe-like height in vertical frame, long toothless beak, prehistoric landscape with ferns and ancient forest, warm amber light, professional documentary grading, National Geographic quality, vertical Stories composition, scientifically accurate, 8K detailed, dramatic scale

usage_notes.for_stable_diffusion: Use main_prompt + detailed_elements, include negative_prompt, photorealistic models, may benefit from paleontology LoRA
usage_notes.for_dalle: Use complete_prompt_string, emphasize vertical portrait orientation and Stories/Reels optimization
stories_reels_tips: Export at 1080x1920px minimum for Instagram/Facebook Stories and Reels
Consider 2160x3840px for TikTok and premium quality
Vertical format naturally emphasizes dinosaur height and scale
Documentary realism creates educational shareable content
Golden hour aesthetic adds cinematic quality
Position key dinosaur elements within safe zones
Test composition on mobile screen for optimal prehistoric impact

for_reels_tiktok: Slow upward camera tilt from foreground ferns to towering T-Rex (Ken Burns effect)
Subtle parallax effect with foreground dinosaur and background landscape
Slow motion of Quetzalcoatlus flight or landing
Focus pull from foreground Triceratops to background T-Rex
Time-lapse style showing golden hour light progression over prehistoric scene
Animated heat haze and atmospheric effects for subtropical realism
